value.displayDescription: Availability percentage with the following calculation: (Total Calls - Server Errors)/Total Calls. Server Errors include any HTTP responses >=500.
Number of calls made to the model API over a period of time. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.

Recommended latency (responsiveness) measure for streaming requests. Applies to PTU and PTU-managed deployments. Calculated as time taken for the first response to appear after a user sends a prompt, as measured by the API gateway. This number increases as the prompt size increases and/or cache hit size reduces. To breakdown time to response metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, and ModelVersion. 

Note: this metric is an approximation as measured latency is heavily dependent on multiple factors, including concurrent calls and overall workload pattern. In addition, it does not account for any client-side latency that may exist between your client and the API endpoint. Please refer to your own logging for optimal latency tracking.
For streaming requests; Model token generation rate, measured in milliseconds. Applies to PTU and PTU-managed deployments.
For streaming and non-streaming requests; time it takes for last byte of response data to be received after request is made by model. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed, and Pay-as-you-go deployments.

For streaming and non-streaming requests; time it takes for first byte of response data to be received after request is made by model, normalized by token. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed, and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
Enumerates the generation speed for a given model response. The total tokens generated is divided by the time to generate the tokens, in seconds. Applies to PTU and PTU-managed deployments.
Number of prompt tokens processed (input) on a model. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
Number of tokens generated (output) from an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
Number of inference tokens processed on a model. Calculated as prompt tokens (input) plus generated tokens (output). Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.

Utilization % for a provisoned-managed deployment, calculated as (PTUs consumed / PTUs deployed) x 100. When utilization is greater than or equal to 100%, calls are throttled and error code 429 returned.
Total number of pages processed. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
Total number of pages processed with annotations. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
Total number of images generated. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
Total number of tokens read from the cache. Applies to Anthropic model deployments. Surfaced in response usage section as `cache_read_input_tokens`

name.localizedValue: Prompt tokens written to cache (5 minute TTL)

value.displayDescription: The number of prompt tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry. Applies to Anthropic model deployments. Surfaced in response usage section as `cache_creation.ephemeral_5m_input_tokens`

name.localizedValue: Prompt tokens written to cache (1 hour TTL)

value.displayDescription: The number of prompt tokens used to create the 1 hour entry. Applies to Anthropic model deployments. Surfaced in response usage section as `cache_creation.ephemeral_1h_input_tokens`
Total number of calls. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.

Number of calls made to the Azure OpenAI API over a period of time. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. To breakdown API requests, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, ModelVersion, StatusCode (successful, clienterrors, server errors), IsSpillover for spillover information, ServiceTier, StreamType (Streaming vs non-streaming requests) and operation.
Availability percentage with the following calculation: (Total Calls - Server Errors)/Total Calls. Server Errors include any HTTP responses >=500.
Number of successful calls. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Total number of calls with error response (HTTP response code 4xx or 5xx). Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Number of calls that exceeded rate or quota limit. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Number of calls with service internal error (HTTP response code 5xx). Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Number of calls with client side error (HTTP response code 4xx). Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Availability percentage with the following calculation: (Total Calls - Server Errors)/Total Calls. Server Errors include any HTTP responses >=500. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Size of incoming data in bytes. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Size of outgoing data in bytes. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Latency in milliseconds. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
The current ratelimit of the ratelimit key. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.

Number of inference tokens processed on an OpenAI model. Calculated as prompt tokens (input) plus generated tokens (output). Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName and ModelName.

Number of prompt tokens processed (input) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName and ModelName.
Number of audio prompt tokens processed (input) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU-managed model deployments.
Number of audio prompt tokens processed (input) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go model deployments.

Number of tokens generated (output) from an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName and ModelName.
Number of audio prompt tokens generated (output) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU-managed model deployments.
Number of audio prompt tokens generated (output) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go model deployments.

Total tokens minus cached tokens over a period of time. Applies to PTU and PTU-managed deployments.  Use this metric to understand your TPS or TPM based utilization for PTUs and compare to your benchmarks for target TPS or TPM for your scenarios. To breakdown API requests, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, and ModelVersion.
Number of Training Hours Processed on an OpenAI FineTuned Model

Utilization % for a provisoned-managed deployment, calculated as (PTUs consumed / PTUs deployed) x 100. When utilization is greater than or equal to 100%, calls are throttled and error code 429 returned. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, ModelVersion and StreamType (Streaming vs non-streaming requests)

Utilization % for a provisoned-managed deployment, calculated as (PTUs consumed / PTUs deployed) x 100. Percentage of prompt tokens that hit the cache. Applies to PTU and PTU-managed deployments.

For streaming and non-streaming requests; time it takes for first byte of response data to be received after request is made by model, normalized by token. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed, and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
For streaming requests; model token generation rate, measured in milliseconds. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.

Enumerates the generation speed for a given Azure OpenAI model response. The total tokens generated is divided by the time to generate the tokens, in seconds. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
For streaming and non-streaming requests; time it takes for last byte of response data to be received after request is made by model. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed, and Pay-as-you-go deployments.

Total number of images generated. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
Total number of tokens read from the cache. Applies to Anthropic model deployments. Surfaced in response usage section as `cache_read_input_tokens`

name.localizedValue: Prompt tokens written to cache (5 minute TTL)

value.displayDescription: The number of prompt tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry. Applies to Anthropic model deployments. Surfaced in response usage section as `cache_creation.ephemeral_5m_input_tokens`

name.localizedValue: Prompt tokens written to cache (1 hour TTL)

value.displayDescription: The number of prompt tokens used to create the 1 hour entry. Applies to Anthropic model deployments. Surfaced in response usage section as `cache_creation.ephemeral_1h_input_tokens`
Total number of calls. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.

Number of calls made to the Azure OpenAI API over a period of time. Applies to PTU, PTU-Managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. To breakdown API requests, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, ModelVersion, StatusCode (successful, clienterrors, server errors), IsSpillover for spillover information, ServiceTier, StreamType (Streaming vs non-streaming requests) and operation.
Availability percentage with the following calculation: (Total Calls - Server Errors)/Total Calls. Server Errors include any HTTP responses >=500.
Number of successful calls. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Total number of calls with error response (HTTP response code 4xx or 5xx). Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Number of calls that exceeded rate or quota limit. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Number of calls with service internal error (HTTP response code 5xx). Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Number of calls with client side error (HTTP response code 4xx). Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Availability percentage with the following calculation: (Total Calls - Server Errors)/Total Calls. Server Errors include any HTTP responses >=500. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Size of incoming data in bytes. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Size of outgoing data in bytes. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
Latency in milliseconds. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.
The current ratelimit of the ratelimit key. Do not use for Azure OpenAI service.

Number of inference tokens processed on an OpenAI model. Calculated as prompt tokens (input) plus generated tokens (output). Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName and ModelName.

Number of prompt tokens processed (input) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName and ModelName.
Number of audio prompt tokens processed (input) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU-managed model deployments.
Number of audio prompt tokens processed (input) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go model deployments.

Number of tokens generated (output) from an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName and ModelName.
Number of audio prompt tokens generated (output) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU-managed model deployments.
Number of audio prompt tokens generated (output) on an OpenAI model. Applies to PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go model deployments.

Total tokens minus cached tokens over a period of time. Applies to PTU and PTU-managed deployments.  Use this metric to understand your TPS or TPM based utilization for PTUs and compare to your benchmarks for target TPS or TPM for your scenarios. To breakdown API requests, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, and ModelVersion.
Number of Training Hours Processed on an OpenAI FineTuned Model

Utilization % for a provisoned-managed deployment, calculated as (PTUs consumed / PTUs deployed) x 100. When utilization is greater than or equal to 100%, calls are throttled and error code 429 returned. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, ModelVersion and StreamType (Streaming vs non-streaming requests)

Utilization % for a provisoned-managed deployment, calculated as (PTUs consumed / PTUs deployed) x 100. When utilization is greater than or equal to 100%, calls are throttled and error code 429 returned. To breakdown this metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, ModelVersion and StreamType (Streaming vs non-streaming requests)

Recommended latency (responsiveness) measure for streaming requests. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments. Calculated as time taken for the first response to appear after a user sends a prompt, as measured by the API gateway. This number increases as the prompt size increases and/or cache hit size reduces. To breakdown time to response metric, you can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName, and ModelVersion. 

Note: this metric is an approximation as measured latency is heavily dependent on multiple factors, including concurrent calls and overall workload pattern. In addition, it does not account for any client-side latency that may exist between your client and the API endpoint. Please refer to your own logging for optimal latency tracking.
Percentage of prompt tokens that hit the cache. Applies to PTU and PTU-managed deployments.

For streaming and non-streaming requests; time it takes for first byte of response data to be received after request is made by model, normalized by token. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed, and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
For streaming requests; model token generation rate, measured in milliseconds. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.

Enumerates the generation speed for a given Azure OpenAI model response. The total tokens generated is divided by the time to generate the tokens, in seconds. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed and Pay-as-you-go deployments.
For streaming and non-streaming requests; time it takes for last byte of response data to be received after request is made by model. Applies to PTU, PTU-managed, and Pay-as-you-go deployments.

name.localizedValue: Total Volume Sent For Safety Check

value.displayDescription: Number of calls made to Azure OpenAI API and detected by content filter applied over a period of time. You can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName.

Number of calls made to Azure OpenAI API and detected as harmful(both block model and annotate mode) by content filter applied over a period of time. You can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName and TextType.

Number of calls made to Azure OpenAI API and rejected by content filter applied over a period of time. You can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimensions: ModelDeploymentName, ModelName and TextType.
Number of potentially abusive user that detected over a period of time. You can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimension: ModelDeploymentName.
System event for risks & safety monitoring. You can add a filter or apply splitting by the following dimension: EventType.
RealtimeAPI number of seconds used
